\\t\\t\\topt(&r.QueryParams)\\n300: \\t\\t}\\n301: \\t}\\n302: }\\n303: \\n304: // PageParameter is any type which exposes limit and page token\\n305: // accessors used to identify pages.\\n306: type {\\n321: \\topts := []QueryOption{\\n322: \\t\\tWithLimit(uint64(p.GetLimit())),\\n323: \\t\\tWithPageToken(p.GetPageToken()),\\n324: \\t}\\n325: \\n326: \\tif po, ok := p.(OffsetPageParameter); ok func(*ListRequest[T])\n\n// ListWithQueryParamOptions takes a set of functional options for QueryParam and returns a ListOption\n// which applies them in order on the provided ListRequest.\nfunc which exposes an additional offset\n// accessor for legacy paging implementations (page token supersedes).\ntype OffsetPageParameter interface {\n\tPageParameter\n\tGetOffset() int32\n}\n\n// ListWithParameters constructs a new ListRequest using the page parameters\n// exposed by the provided PageParameter implementation.\nfunc ListWithParameters[T any](t T, p PageParameter) *ListRequest[T] {\n\topts := []QueryOption{\n\t\tWithLimit(uint64(p.GetLimit())),\n\t\tWithPageToken(p.GetPageToken()),\n\t}\n\n\tif po, ok := p.(OffsetPageParameter); ok {\n\t\topts = append(opts, WithOffset(uint64(po.GetOffset())))\n\t}\n\n\treturn ListWithOptions[T](t, ListWithQueryParamOptions[T](opts...))\n}\n\n// ListWithOptions constructs a new ListRequest using the provided req\n}\n\n// Reference is a string which can refer to either a concrete\n// revision or it can be an indirect named reference.\ntype Reference string\n\n// ReferenceRequest is used to identify a request predicated solely by a revision reference.\n// This is primarily used for namespaces as it is the highest level domain model.\ntype ReferenceRequest struct {\n\tReference\n}\n\n// s.evaluator.matchConstraints(r.Context, v.Constraints, v.MatchType, r.EntityId)\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// if we don't match the segment, fall through to the next rollout.\n\t\t\t\tif matched {\n\t\t\t\t\ts.logger.Debug(reason)\n\t\t\t\t\tsegmentMatches++\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tswitch rollout.Segment.SegmentOperator {\n\t\t\tcase flipt.SegmentOperator_OR_SEGMENT_OPERATOR:\n\t\t\t\tif segmentMatches < 1 {\n\t\t\t\t\ts.logger.Debug(\"did not match ANY segments\")\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcase flipt.SegmentOperator_AND_SEGMENT_OPERATOR:\n\t\t\t\tif len(rollout.Segment.Segments) != segmentMatches {\n\t\t\t\t\ts.logger.Debug(\"did not match ALL segments\")\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tresp.Enabled = rollout.Segment.Value\n\t\t\tresp.Reason = rpcevaluation.EvaluationReason_MATCH_EVALUATION_REASON\n\t\t\tresp.FlagKey = flag.Key\n\n\t\t\ts.logger.Debug(\"segment based matched\", zap.Int(\"rank\", int(rollout.Rank)), zap.Strings(\"segments\", segmentKeys))\n\t\t\treturn resp, nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If we have exhausted all rollouts and we still don't have a match, return flag enabled value.\n\tresp.Reason = rpcevaluation.EvaluationReason_DEFAULT_EVALUATION_REASON\n\tresp.Enabled = flag.Enabled\n\tresp.FlagKey = flag.Key\n\n\ts.logger.Debug(\"default rollout matched\", zap.Bool(\"enabled\", flag.Enabled))\n\treturn resp, nil\n}\n\n// Batch takes in a list of *evaluation.EvaluationRequest and returns their respective responses.\nfunc (s *Server) Batch(ctx context.Context, b *rpcevaluation.BatchEvaluationRequest) (*rpcevaluation.BatchEvaluationResponse, error) {\n\tresp := &rpcevaluation.BatchEvaluationResponse{\n\t\tResponses: make([]*rpcevaluation.EvaluationResponse, 0, len(b.Requests)),\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, req := range b.GetRequests() {\n\t\tf, err &rpcevaluation.EvaluationResponse_ErrorResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tErrorResponse: &rpcevaluation.ErrorEvaluationResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tFlagKey:      req.FlagKey,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tNamespaceKey: req.NamespaceKey,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tReason:       rpcevaluation.ErrorEvaluationReason_NOT_FOUND_ERROR_EVALUATION_REASON,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tresp.Responses = append(resp.Responses, eresp)\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tswitch f.Type {\n\t\tcase flipt.FlagType_BOOLEAN_FLAG_TYPE:\n\t\t\tres, err := s.boolean(ctx, f, rpcevaluation.EvaluationResponseType_BOOLEAN_EVALUATION_RESPONSE_TYPE,\n\t\t\t\tResponse: &rpcevaluation.EvaluationResponse_BooleanResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\tBooleanResponse: res,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tresp.Responses = append(resp.Responses, eresp)\n\t\tcase flipt.FlagType_VARIANT_FLAG_TYPE:\n\t\t\tres, err := s.variant(ctx, f, req)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\teresp := &rpcevaluation.EvaluationResponse{\n\t\t\t\tType: rpcevaluation.EvaluationResponseType_VARIANT_EVALUATION_RESPONSE_TYPE,\n\t\t\t\tResponse: \\treturn resp, nil\\n221: }\\n222: \\n223: // matchConstraints is a utility function that will return if all or any constraints have matched for a segment depending\\n224: // on the match \\n355: \\treturn false\\n356: }\\n357: \\n358: func matchesNumber(c storage.EvaluationConstraint, v string) (bool, error) {\\n359: \\tswitch c.Operator {\\n360: \\tcase flipt.OpNotPresent:\\n361: NewEvaluator(logger *zap.Logger, store Storer) *Evaluator {\n32: \treturn &Evaluator{\n33: \t\tlogger: logger,\n34: \t\tstore:  store,\n35: \t}\n36: }\n37: \n38: func (e *Evaluator) Evaluate(ctx context.Context, flag *flipt.Flag, r *evaluation.EvaluationRequest) (resp *flipt.EvaluationResponse, err error) {\n39: \t// Flag should be of variant type by default. However, for maximum backwards compatibility\n40: \t// on this layer, we will check against the integer value of the flag type.\n41: \tif int(flag.Type) != 0 || flag.Type != flipt.FlagType_VARIANT_FLAG_TYPE {\n42: \t\tresp = &flipt.EvaluationResponse{}\n43: \t\tresp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_ERROR_EVALUATION_REASON\n44: \t\treturn resp, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"flag type %s invalid\", flag.Type)\n45: \t}\n46: \n47: \tvar (\n48: \t\tstartTime     = time.Now().UTC()\n49: \t\tnamespaceAttr = metrics.AttributeNamespace.String(r.NamespaceKey)\n50: \t\tflagAttr      = metrics.AttributeFlag.String(r.FlagKey)\n51: \t)\n52: \n53: \tmetrics.EvaluationsTotal.Add(ctx, 1, metric.WithAttributeSet(attribute.NewSet(namespaceAttr, flagAttr)))\n54: \n55: \tdefer func() {\n56: \t\tif err == nil {\n57: \t\t\tmetrics.EvaluationResultsTotal.Add(ctx, 1,\n58: \t\t\t\tmetric.WithAttributeSet(\n59: \t\t\t\t\tattribute.NewSet(\n60: \t\t\t\t\t\tnamespaceAttr,\n61: \t\t\t\t\t\tflagAttr,\n62: \t\t\t\t\t\tmetrics.AttributeMatch.Bool(resp.Match),\n63: \t\t\t\t\t\tmetrics.AttributeSegment.String(resp.SegmentKey),\n64: \t\t\t\t\t\tmetrics.AttributeReason.String(resp.Reason.String()),\n65: \t\t\t\t\t\tmetrics.AttributeValue.String(resp.Value),\n66: \t\t\t\t\t\tmetrics.AttributeType.String(\"variant\"),\n67: \t\t\t\t\t),\n68: \t\t\t\t),\n69: \t\t\t)\n70: \t\t} else {\n71: \t\t\tmetrics.EvaluationErrorsTotal.Add(ctx, 1, metric.WithAttributeSet(attribute.NewSet(namespaceAttr, flagAttr)))\n72: \t\t}\n73: \n74: \t\tmetrics.EvaluationLatency.Record(\n75: \t\t\tctx,\n76: \t\t\tfloat64(time.Since(startTime).Nanoseconds())/1e6,\n77: \t\t\tmetric.WithAttributeSet(\n78: \t\t\t\tattribute.NewSet(\n79: \t\t\t\t\tnamespaceAttr,\n80: \t\t\t\t\tflagAttr,\n81: \t\t\t\t),\n82: \t\t\t),\n83: \t\t)\n84: \t}()\n85: \n86: \tresp = &flipt.EvaluationResponse{\n87: \t\tRequestId:      r.RequestId,\n88: \t\tEntityId:       r.EntityId,\n89: \t\tRequestContext: r.Context,\n90: \t\tFlagKey:        r.FlagKey,\n91: \t\tNamespaceKey:   r.NamespaceKey,\n92: \t}\n93: \n94: \tif !flag.Enabled {\n95: \t\tresp.Match = false\n96: \t\tresp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_FLAG_DISABLED_EVALUATION_REASON\n97: \t\treturn resp, nil\n98: \t}\n99: \n100: \trules, err := e.store.GetEvaluationRules(ctx, storage.NewResource(r.NamespaceKey, r.FlagKey))\n101: \tif err != nil {\n102: \t\tresp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_ERROR_EVALUATION_REASON\n103: \t\treturn resp, err\n104: \t}\n105: \n106: \tif len(rules) == 0 {\n107: \t\te.logger.Debug(\"no rules match\")\n108: \t\treturn resp, nil\n109: \t}\n110: \n111: \tvar lastRank int32\n112: \n113: \t// rule loop\n114: \tfor _, rule := range rules {\n115: \t\tif rule.Rank < lastRank {\n116: \t\t\tresp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_ERROR_EVALUATION_REASON\n117: \t\t\treturn resp, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"rule rank: %d detected out of order\", rule.Rank)\n118: \t\t}\n119: \n120: \t\tlastRank = rule.Rank\n121: \n122: \t\tsegmentKeys := make([]string, 0, len(rule.Segments))\n123: \t\tsegmentMatches := 0\n124: \n125: \t\tfor k, v := range segmentMatches < 1 {\n142: \t\t\t\te.logger.Debug(\"did not match ANY segments\")\n143: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n144: \t\t\t}\n145: \t\tcase flipt.SegmentOperator_AND_SEGMENT_OPERATOR:\n146: \t\t\tif len(rule.Segments) != segmentMatches {\n147: \t\t\t\te.logger.Debug(\"did not match ALL segments\")\n148: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n149: \t\t\t}\n150: \t\t}\n151: \n152: \t\t// For legacy reasons of supporting SegmentKey.\n153: \t\t// The old EvaluationResponse will return both the SegmentKey, and SegmentKeys.\n154: \t\t// If there are multiple segmentKeys, the old EvaluationResponse will take the first value\n155: \t\t// in the segmentKeys slice.\n156: \t\tif len(segmentKeys) > 0 {\n157: \t\t\tresp.SegmentKey = segmentKeys[0]\n158: \t\t\tresp.SegmentKeys = segmentKeys\n159: \t\t}\n160: \n161: \t\tdistributions, err := e.store.GetEvaluationDistributions(ctx, storage.NewID(rule.ID))\n162: \t\tif err != nil {\n163: \t\t\tresp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_ERROR_EVALUATION_REASON\n164: \t\t\treturn resp, err\n165: \t\t}\n166: \n167: \t\tvar (\n168: \t\t\tvalidDistributions []*storage.EvaluationDistribution\n169: \t\t\tbuckets            []int\n170: \t\t)\n171: \n172: \t\tfor _, d := range distributions {\n173: \t\t\t// don't include 0% rollouts\n174: \t\t\tif d.Rollout > 0 {\n175: \t\t\t\tvalidDistributions = append(validDistributions, d)\n176: \n177: \t\t\t\tif buckets == nil {\n178: \t\t\t\t\tbucket := int(d.Rollout * percentMultiplier)\n179: \t\t\t\t\tbuckets = append(buckets, bucket)\n180: \t\t\t\t} else {\n181: \t\t\t\t\tbucket := buckets[len(buckets)-1] + int(d.Rollout*percentMultiplier)\n182: \t\t\t\t\tbuckets = append(buckets, bucket)\n183: \t\t\t\t}\n184: \t\t\t}\n185: \t\t}\n186: \n187: \t\t// no distributions for rule\n188: \t\tif len(validDistributions) == 0 {\n189: \t\t\te.logger.Info(\"no distributions for rule\")\n190: \t\t\tresp.Match = true\n191: \t\t\tresp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_MATCH_EVALUATION_REASON\n192: \t\t\treturn resp, nil\n193: \t\t}\n194: \n195: \t\tvar (\n196: \t\t\tbucket = crc32Num(r.EntityId, r.FlagKey)\n197: \t\t\t// sort.SearchInts searches for x in a sorted slice of ints and returns the index\n198: \t\t\t// as specified by Search. The return value is the index to insert x if x is\n199: \t\t\t// not present (it could be len(a)).\n200: \t\t\tindex = sort.SearchInts(buckets, int(bucket)+1)\n201: \t\t)\n202: \n203: \t\t// if index is outside of our existing buckets then it does not match any distribution\n204: \t\tif index == len(validDistributions) {\n205: \t\t\tresp.Match = false\n206: \t\t\te.logger.Debug(\"did not match any distributions\")\n207: \t\t\treturn resp, nil\n208: \t\t}\n209: \n210: \t\td := validDistributions[index]\n211: \t\te.logger.Debug(\"matched distribution\", zap.Reflect(\"evaluation_distribution\", d))\n212: \n213: \t\tresp.Match = true\n214: \t\tresp.Value = d.VariantKey\n215: \t\tresp.Attachment = d.VariantAttachment\n216: \t\tresp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_MATCH_EVALUATION_REASON\n217: \t\treturn resp, nil\n218: \t} // end rule loop\n219: \n220: \treturn resp, nil\n221: }\n222: \n223: // matchConstraints is a utility function that will return if all or any constraints have matched for a segment depending\n224: // on the match type.\n225: func (e *Evaluator) matchConstraints(evalCtx map[string]string, constraints []storage.EvaluationConstraint, segmentMatchType flipt.MatchType, entityId string) (bool, string, error) {\n226: \tconstraintMatches := 0\n227: \n228: \tvar reason string\n229: \n230: \tfor _, c := range constraints {\n231: \t\tv := evalCtx[c.Property]\n232: \n233: \t\tvar (\n234: \t\t\tmatch bool\n235: \t\t\terr   error\n236: \t\t)\n237: \n238: \t\tswitch c.Type {\n239: \t\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_STRING_COMPARISON_TYPE:\n240: \t\t\tmatch = matchesString(c, matchesBool(c, v)\n245: \t\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_DATETIME_COMPARISON_TYPE:\n246: \t\t\tmatch, err = matchesDateTime(c, v)\n247: \t\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_ENTITY_ID_COMPARISON_TYPE:\n248: \t\t\tmatch = matchesString(c, entityId)\n249: \t\tdefault:\n250: \t\t\treturn false, reason, errs.ErrInvalid(\"unknown constraint type\")\n251: \t\t}\n252: \n253: \t\tif err != nil {\n254: \t\t\te.logger.Debug(\"error matching constraint\", zap.String(\"property\", c.Property), zap.Error(err))\n255: \t\t\t// don't return here because we want to continue to evaluate the other constraints\n256: \t\t}\n257: \n258: \t\tif match {\n259: \t\t\t// increase the matchCount\n260: \t\t\tconstraintMatches++\n261: \n262: \t\t\tswitch segmentMatchType {\n263: \t\t\tcase flipt.MatchType_ANY_MATCH_TYPE:\n264: \t\t\t\t// can short circuit here since we had at least one match\n265: \t\t\t\tbreak\n266: \t\t\tdefault:\n267: \t\t\t\t// keep looping as we need to match all constraints\n268: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n269: \t\t\t}\n270: \t\t} else {\n271: \t\t\t// no match\n272: \t\t\tswitch segmentMatchType {\n273: \t\t\tcase flipt.MatchType_ALL_MATCH_TYPE:\n274: \t\t\t\t// we can short circuit because we must match all constraints\n275: \t\t\t\tbreak\n276: \t\t\tdefault:\n277: \t\t\t\t// keep looping to see if we match the next constraint\n278: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n279: \t\t\t}\n280: \t\t}\n281: \t}\n282: \n283: \tvar matched = true\n284: \n285: \tswitch segmentMatchType {\n286: \tcase flipt.MatchType_ALL_MATCH_TYPE:\n287: \t\tif len(constraints) != constraintMatches {\n288: \t\t\treason = \"did not match ALL constraints\"\n289: \t\t\tmatched = false\n290: \t\t}\n291: \n292: \tcase flipt.MatchType_ANY_MATCH_TYPE:\n293: \t\tif len(constraints) > 0 && constraintMatches == 0 {\n294: \t\t\treason = \"did not match ANY constraints\"\n295: \t\t\tmatched = false\n296: \t\t}\n297: \tdefault:\n298: \t\treason = fmt.Sprintf(\"unknown match type %d\", segmentMatchType)\n299: \t\tmatched = false\n300: \t}\n301: \n302: \treturn matched, reason, nil\n303: }\n304: \n305: func crc32Num(entityID string, salt string) uint {\n306: \treturn uint(crc32.ChecksumIEEE([]byte(salt+entityID))) % totalBucketNum\n307: }\n308: \n309: const (\n310: \t// totalBucketNum represents how many buckets we can use to determine the consistent hashing\n311: \t// distribution and rollout\n312: \ttotalBucketNum uint = 1000\n313: \n314: \t// percentMultiplier implies that the multiplier between percentage (100) and totalBucketNum\n315: \tpercentMultiplier float32 = float32(totalBucketNum) / 100\n316: )\n317: \n318: func matchesString(c storage.EvaluationConstraint, v string) bool {\n319: \tswitch c.Operator {\n320: \tcase flipt.OpEmpty:\n321: \t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) == 0\n322: \tcase flipt.OpNotEmpty:\n323: \t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) != 0\n324: \t}\n325: \n326: \tif v == \"\" {\n327: \t\treturn false\n328: \t}\n329: \n330: \tvalue := c.Value\n331: \n332: \tswitch c.Operator {\n333: \tcase flipt.OpEQ:\n334: \t\treturn value == v\n335: \tcase flipt.OpNEQ:\n336: \t\treturn value != v\n337: \tcase flipt.OpIsOneOf:\n342: \t\tvalues := []string{}\n343: \t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(value), &values); err != nil {\n344: \t\t\treturn false\n345: \t\t}\n346: \t\treturn slices.Contains(values, v)\n347: \tcase flipt.OpIsNotOneOf:\n348: \t\tvalues := []string{}\n349: \t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(value), &values); err != nil {\n350: \t\t\treturn false\n351: \t\t}\n352: \t\treturn !slices.Contains(values, v)\n353: \t}\n354: \n355: \treturn false\n356: }\n357: \n358: func matchesNumber(c storage.EvaluationConstraint, v string) (bool, error) {\n359: \tswitch c.Operator {\n360: \tcase flipt.OpNotPresent:\n361: \t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) == 0, nil\n362: \tcase flipt.OpPresent:\n363: \t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) != 0, nil\n364: \t}\n365: \n366: \t// can't %q\", c.Value)\n394: \t}\n395: \n396: \tswitch c.Operator {\n397: \tcase flipt.OpEQ:\n398: \t\treturn value == n, nil\n399: \tcase flipt.OpNEQ:\n400: \t\treturn value != n, nil\n401: \tcase \t\treturn n >= value, nil\n409: \t}\n410: \n411: \treturn false, nil\n412: }\n413: \n414: func matchesBool(c storage.EvaluationConstraint, v string) (bool, error) {\n415: \tswitch c.Operator {\n416: flipt.OpNotPresent:\n445: \t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) == 0, nil\n446: \tcase flipt.OpPresent:\n447: \t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) != 0, nil\n448: \t}\n449: \n450: \t// can't parse an flipt.OpEmpty:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) == 0\n\tcase flipt.OpNotEmpty:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) != 0\n\t}\n\n\tif v == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tvalue := c.Value\n\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpEQ:\n\t\treturn value == v\n\tcase flipt.OpNEQ:\n\t\treturn value != v\n\tcase flipt.OpPrefix:\n\t\treturn strings.HasPrefix(strings.TrimSpace(v), value)\n\tcase flipt.OpSuffix:\n\t\treturn strings.HasSuffix(strings.TrimSpace(v), value)\n\tcase flipt.OpIsOneOf:\n\t\tvalues := []string{}\n\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(value), &values); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn slices.Contains(values, v)\n\tcase flipt.OpIsNotOneOf:\n\t\tvalues := []string{}\n\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(value), &values); err != nil flipt.OpPresent:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) != 0, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// can't parse an empty string\n\tif v == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false, nil\n\t}\n\n\tvalue, err := strconv.ParseBool(v)\n\tif lastRank {\n\t\t\tresp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_ERROR_EVALUATION_REASON\n\t\t\treturn resp, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"rule rank: %d detected out of order\", rule.Rank)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tlastRank = rule.Rank\n\n\t\tsegmentKeys := make([]string, 0, len(rule.Segments))\n\t\tsegmentMatches := 0\n\n\t\tfor k, v := range rule.Segments {\n\t\t\tmatched, reason, err := e.matchConstraints(r.Context, v.Constraints, v.MatchType, r.EntityId)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tresp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_ERROR_EVALUATION_REASON\n\t\t\t\treturn resp, err\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif matched {\n\t\t\t\te.logger.Debug(reason)\n\t\t\t\tsegmentKeys = append(segmentKeys, k)\n\t\t\t\tsegmentMatches++\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tswitch rule.SegmentOperator {\n\t\tcase flipt.SegmentOperator_OR_SEGMENT_OPERATOR:\n\t\t\tif segmentMatches < 1 {\n\t\t\t\te.logger.Debug(\"did not match ANY segments\")\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase flipt.SegmentOperator_AND_SEGMENT_OPERATOR:\n\t\t\tif len(rule.Segments) != segmentMatches {\n\t\t\t\te.logger.Debug(\"did not match ALL segments\")\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// For legacy reasons of supporting SegmentKey.\n\t\t// The old EvaluationResponse will return both the SegmentKey, and SegmentKeys.\n\t\t// If there are multiple segmentKeys, the old EvaluationResponse will take the first value\n\t\t// in the segmentKeys slice.\n\t\tif len(segmentKeys) > 0 {\n\t\t\tresp.SegmentKey = segmentKeys[0]\n\t\t\tresp.SegmentKeys = segmentKeys\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdistributions, err := e.store.GetEvaluationDistributions(ctx, storage.NewID(rule.ID))\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tresp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_ERROR_EVALUATION_REASON\n\t\t\treturn resp, err\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvar (\n\t\t\tvalidDistributions []*storage.EvaluationDistribution\n\t\t\tbuckets            []int\n\t\t)\n\n\t\tfor _, d := range distributions {\n\t\t\t// don't include 0% rollouts\n\t\t\tif d.Rollout > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tvalidDistributions = append(validDistributions, d)\n\n\t\t\t\tif buckets == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tbucket := int(d.Rollout * percentMultiplier)\n\t\t\t\t\tbuckets = append(buckets, bucket)\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tbucket := buckets[len(buckets)-1] + int(d.Rollout*percentMultiplier)\n\t\t\t\t\tbuckets = append(buckets, bucket)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// no distributions for rule\n\t\tif len(validDistributions) == 0 {\n\t\t\te.logger.Info(\"no distributions for rule\")\n\t\t\tresp.Match = true\n\t\t\tresp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_MATCH_EVALUATION_REASON\n\t\t\treturn resp, nil\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvar (\n\t\t\tbucket = crc32Num(r.EntityId, r.FlagKey)\n\t\t\t// sort.SearchInts searches for x in a sorted slice of ints and returns the index\n\t\t\t// as specified by Search. The return value is the index to insert x if x is\n\t\t\t// not present (it could be len(a)).\n\t\t\tindex = sort.SearchInts(buckets, int(bucket)+1)\n\t\t)\n\n\t\t// if index is outside of our existing buckets then it does not match any distribution\n\t\tif index == len(validDistributions) {\n\t\t\tresp.Match = false\n\t\t\te.logger.Debug(\"did not match any distributions\")\n\t\t\treturn resp, nil\n\t\t}\n\n\t\td := validDistributions[index]\n\t\te.logger.Debug(\"matched distribution\", zap.Reflect(\"evaluation_distribution\", d))\n\n\t\tresp.Match = true\n\t\tresp.Value = d.VariantKey\n\t\tresp.Attachment = d.VariantAttachment\n\t\tresp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_MATCH_EVALUATION_REASON\n\t\treturn resp, nil\n\t} // end rule loop\n\n\treturn resp, nil\n}\n\n// matchConstraints is a utility function that will return if all or any constraints have matched for a segment depending\n// on the match type.\nfunc (e *Evaluator) matchConstraints(evalCtx map[string]string, constraints []storage.EvaluationConstraint, segmentMatchType flipt.MatchType, entityId string) (bool, string, error) {\n\tconstraintMatches := 0\n\n\tvar reason string\n\n\tfor _, c := range constraints {\n\t\tv := evalCtx[c.Property]\n\n\t\tvar (\n\t\t\tmatch bool\n\t\t\terr   error\n\t\t)\n\n\t\tswitch c.Type {\n\t\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_STRING_COMPARISON_TYPE:\n\t\t\tmatch = matchesString(c, v)\n\t\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_NUMBER_COMPARISON_TYPE:\n\t\t\tmatch, err = matchesNumber(c, v)\n\t\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_BOOLEAN_COMPARISON_TYPE:\n\t\t\tmatch, err = matchesBool(c, v)\n\t\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_DATETIME_COMPARISON_TYPE:\n\t\t\tmatch, err = matchesDateTime(c, v)\n\t\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_ENTITY_ID_COMPARISON_TYPE:\n\t\t\tmatch = matchesString(c, entityId)\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\treturn false, reason, errs.ErrInvalid(\"unknown constraint type\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\te.logger.Debug(\"error matching constraint\", zap.String(\"property\", c.Property), zap.Error(err))\n\t\t\t// don't return here because we want to continue to evaluate the other constraints\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif match {\n\t\t\t// increase the matchCount\n\t\t\tconstraintMatches++\n\n\t\t\tswitch segmentMatchType {\n\t\t\tcase flipt.MatchType_ANY_MATCH_TYPE:\n\t\t\t\t// can short circuit here since we had at least one match\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\t// keep looping as we need to match all constraints\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// no match\n\t\t\tswitch segmentMatchType {\n\t\t\tcase flipt.MatchType_ALL_MATCH_TYPE:\n\t\t\t\t// we can short circuit because we must match all constraints\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\t// keep looping to see if we match the next constraint\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tvar matched = true\n\n\tswitch segmentMatchType {\n\tcase flipt.MatchType_ALL_MATCH_TYPE:\n\t\tif len(constraints) != constraintMatches {\n\t\t\treason = \"did not match ALL constraints\"\n\t\t\tmatched = false\n\t\t}\n\n\tcase flipt.MatchType_ANY_MATCH_TYPE:\n\t\tif len(constraints) > 0 && constraintMatches == 0 {\n\t\t\treason = \"did not match ANY constraints\"\n\t\t\tmatched = false\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\treason = fmt.Sprintf(\"unknown match type %d\", segmentMatchType)\n\t\tmatched = false\n\t}\n\n\treturn matched, reason, nil\n}\n\nfunc crc32Num(entityID string, salt string) uint {\n\treturn uint(crc32.ChecksumIEEE([]byte(salt+entityID))) % totalBucketNum\n}\n\nconst (\n\t// totalBucketNum represents how many buckets we can use to determine the consistent hashing\n\t// distribution and rollout\n\ttotalBucketNum uint = 1000\n\n\t// percentMultiplier implies that the multiplier between percentage (100) and totalBucketNum\n\tpercentMultiplier float32 = float32(totalBucketNum) / 100\n)\n\nfunc matchesString(c storage.EvaluationConstraint, v string) bool {\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpEmpty:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) == 0\n\tcase flipt.OpNotEmpty:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) != 0\n\t}\n\n\tif v == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tvalue := c.Value\n\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpEQ:\n\t\treturn value == v\n\tcase flipt.OpNEQ:\n\t\treturn value != v\n\tcase flipt.OpPrefix:\n\t\treturn strings.HasPrefix(strings.TrimSpace(v), value)\n\tcase flipt.OpSuffix:\n\t\treturn strings.HasSuffix(strings.TrimSpace(v), value)\n\tcase flipt.OpIsOneOf:\n\t\tvalues := []string{}\n\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(value), &values); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn slices.Contains(values, v)\n\tcase flipt.OpIsNotOneOf:\n\t\tvalues := []string{}\n\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(value), &values); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn !slices.Contains(values, v)\n\t}\n\n\treturn false\n}\n\nfunc matchesNumber(c storage.EvaluationConstraint, v string) (bool, error) {\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpNotPresent:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) == 0, nil\n\tcase flipt.OpPresent:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) != 0, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// can't parse an empty string\n\tif v == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false, nil\n\t}\n\n\tn, err := strconv.ParseFloat(v, 64)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"parsing number from %q\", v)\n\t}\n\n\tif c.Operator == flipt.OpIsOneOf {\n\t\tvalues := []float64{}\n\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(c.Value), &values); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn false, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"Invalid value for constraint %q\", c.Value)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn slices.Contains(values, n), nil\n\t} else if c.Operator == flipt.OpIsNotOneOf {\n\t\tvalues := []float64{}\n\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(c.Value), &values); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn false, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"Invalid value for constraint %q\", c.Value)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn !slices.Contains(values, n), nil\n\t}\n\n\t// TODO: we should consider parsing this at creation time since it doesn't change and it doesnt make sense to allow invalid constraint values\n\tvalue, err := strconv.ParseFloat(c.Value, 64)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"parsing number from %q\", c.Value)\n\t}\n\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpEQ:\n\t\treturn value == n, nil\n\tcase flipt.OpNEQ:\n\t\treturn value != n, nil\n\tcase flipt.OpLT:\n\t\treturn n < value, nil\n\tcase flipt.OpLTE:\n\t\treturn n <= value, nil\n\tcase flipt.OpGT:\n\t\treturn n > value, nil\n\tcase flipt.OpGTE:\n\t\treturn n >= value, nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn false, nil\n}\n\nfunc matchesBool(c storage.EvaluationConstraint, v string) (bool, error) {\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpNotPresent:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) == 0, nil\n\tcase flipt.OpPresent:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) != 0, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// can't parse an empty string\n\tif v == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false, nil\n\t}\n\n\tvalue, err := strconv.ParseBool(v)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"parsing boolean from %q\", v)\n\t}\n\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpTrue:\n\t\treturn value, nil\n\tcase flipt.OpFalse:\n\t\treturn !value, nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn false, nil\n}\n\nfunc matchesDateTime(c storage.EvaluationConstraint, v string) (bool, error) {\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpNotPresent:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) == 0, nil\n\tcase flipt.OpPresent:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) != 0, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// can't parse an empty string\n\tif v == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false, nil\n\t}\n\n\td, err := tryParseDateTime(v)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, err\n\t}\n\n\tvalue, err := tryParseDateTime(c.Value)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, err\n\t}\n\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpEQ:\n\t\treturn value.Equal(d), nil\n\tcase flipt.OpNEQ:\n\t\treturn !value.Equal(d), nil\n\tcase flipt.OpLT:\n\t\treturn d.Before(value), nil\n\tcase flipt.OpLTE:\n\t\treturn d.Before(value) || value.Equal(d), nil\n\tcase flipt.OpGT:\n\t\treturn d.After(value), nil\n\tcase flipt.OpGTE:\n\t\treturn d.After(value) || value.Equal(d), nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn false, nil\n}\n\nfunc tryParseDateTime(v string) (time.Time, error) {\n\tif d, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, v); err == nil {\n\t\treturn d.UTC(), nil\n\t}\n\n\tif d, err := time.Parse(time.DateOnly, v); err == nil {\n\t\treturn d.UTC(), nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn time.Time{}, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"parsing datetime from %q\", v)\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

TestListFlagsPagination_LimitWithNextPage() {\n266: \tt := s.T()\n267: \n268: \treqs := []*flipt.CreateFlagRequest{\n269: \t\t{\n270: \t\t\tKey:         uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()).String(),\n271: pageToken.Key)\n341: \tassert.NotZero(t, pageToken.Offset)\n342: \n343: \treq.QueryParams.Limit = 1\n344: \treq.QueryParams.Order = storage.OrderDesc\n345: \treq.QueryParams.PageToken = \n375: \tnamespace := uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()).String()\n376: \n377: \tctx := context.Background()\n378: \t_, err := s.store.CreateNamespace(ctx, &flipt.CreateNamespaceRequest{\n379: \t\tKey: namespace,\n380: \t})\n381: \trequire.NoError(t, err)\n382: \n383: \tvar (\n384: \t\ttotalFlags = 9\n385: \t\tpageSize   = uint64(3)\n386: \t)\n387: \n388: \tfor i := 0; i < totalFlags; i++ {\n389: \t\treq := flipt.CreateFlagRequest{\n390: \t\t\tNamespaceKey: namespace,\n391: \t\t\tKey:          fmt.Sprintf(\"flag_%03d\", i),\n392: \t\t\tName:         \"foo\",\n393: \t\t\tDescription:  \"bar\",\n394: \t\t}\n395: \n396: \t\t_, err := s.store.CreateFlag(ctx, &req)\n397: \t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n398: \n399: \t\tfor i := 0; i < 2; i++ {\n400: \t\t\tif i > 0 && s.db.Driver == fliptsql.MySQL {\n401: \t\t\t\t// required for MySQL since it only s.stores timestamps to the second and not millisecond granularity\n402: \t\t\t\ttime.Sleep(time.Second)\n403: \t\t\t}\n404: \n405: \t\t\t_, err := s.store.CreateVariant(ctx, &flipt.CreateVariantRequest{\n406: \t\t\t\tNamespaceKey: namespace,\n407: \t\t\t\tFlagKey:      req.Key,\n408: \t\t\t\tKey:          fmt.Sprintf(\"variant_%d\", i),\n409: \t\t\t})\n410: \t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n411: \t\t}\n412: \t}\n413: \n414: \treq := storage.ListWithOptions(\n415: \t\tstorage.NewNamespace(namespace),\n416: \t\tstorage.ListWithQueryParamOptions[storage.NamespaceRequest](\n417: \t\t\tstorage.WithLimit(pageSize),\n418: \t\t),\n419: \t)\n420: \tresp, err := s.store.ListFlags(ctx, req)\n421: \trequire.NoError(t, err)\n422: \n423: \tfound := resp.Results\n424: \tfor token := resp.NextPageToken; token != \"\"; token = resp.NextPageToken {\n425: \t\treq.QueryParams.PageToken = token\n426: \t\tresp, err = s.store.ListFlags(ctx, req)\n427: \t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n428: \n429: \t\tfound = append(found, resp.Results...)\n430: \t}\n431: \n432: \trequire.Len(t, found, totalFlags)\n433: \n434: \tfor i := 0; i < totalFlags; i++ {\n435: \t\tassert.Equal(t, namespace, found[i].NamespaceKey)\n436: \n437: \t\texpectedFlag := fmt.Sprintf(\"flag_%03d\", i)\n438: \t\tassert.Equal(t, expectedFlag, found[i].Key)\n439: \t\tassert.Equal(t, \"foo\", found[i].Name)\n440: \t\tassert.Equal(t, \"bar\", found[i].Description)\n441: \n442: \t\trequire.Len(t, found[i].Variants, 2)\n443: \t\tassert.Equal(t, namespace, found[i].Variants[0].NamespaceKey)\n444: \t\tassert.Equal(t, expectedFlag, found[i].Variants[0].FlagKey)\n445: \t\tassert.Equal(t, \"variant_0\", found[i].Variants[0].Key)\n446: \n447: \t\tassert.Equal(t, namespace, found[i].Variants[1].NamespaceKey)\n448: \t\tassert.Equal(t, expectedFlag, found[i].Variants[1].FlagKey)\n449: \t\tassert.Equal(t, \"variant_1\", found[i].Variants[1].Key)\n450: \t}\n451: }\n452: \n453: func (s *DBTestSuite) TestCreateFlag() {\n454: \tt := s.T()\n455: \n456: \tflag, err := s.store.CreateFlag(context.TODO(),
